title: Ventura Sidebar · Epic Hero in Live Preview
Smart-resize · Glass
summary: Smart-resize IA where the secondary pane is a single frosted-glass surface tinted from the iframe's ambient color; the iframe is the only solid surface. The momentum gauge is etched into the glass at the seam. SR + GLS composed; no other persistent chrome surface.

title: Smart resize · Sites corner
summary: Smart-resize between iframe and one secondary pane; a frosted Sites widget in the top-left corner shows live status (current site + state) AND is the launcher door. Click the widget → the secondary pane swaps into Sites mode and the gauge snaps to 40/60 in the pane's favor. The seam carries a momentum gauge so the IA stays legible.

title: DevTools dock · smart resize
summary: Slim DevTools-style top bar (44px, status-pill saved-state). Below it, smart-resize between iframe (left) and a DevTools-style right dock that swaps Files / Blueprint / Database / Logs / Settings. The seam carries a momentum gauge; the dock collapses to a vertical icon strip at 10% when iframe is in focus, blooms to 65% when the dock is engaged.

title: Smart-resize × Epic recede
summary: Two-pane smart-resize where the secondary pane IS the cinematic Epic launcher at 50/50 when idle, then auto-recedes to 25% the instant a site boots.

title: Tabs on the Seam
summary: Smart-resize is the IA, and the tab strip that swaps the secondary pane's content lives ON the divider between the two panes. The seam carries the tabs AND the momentum gauge — picking what content and weighting which pane happen in the same physical place. Single 44px DEV-style top bar, one Sites corner widget, no rail. Six secondary sections (Sites/Settings/Files/Blueprint/Database/Logs) all reached from the one seam tab strip.

title: Quiet rail · depth tiers
summary: QR (quiet-rail-to-loud IA) + 3LD (three-layer-depth visual). Four quantified blur tiers: iframe 0px, rail 8px, pane 18px, Library overlay 36px. Depth is state — the further from iframe focus, the more blurred. Composes a single 64px rail, one secondary pane swapped by 3- and 5-way segmented controls, a corner Sites widget, a slim DEV-style top capsule, and a mobile mirror that rotates the rail 90° to a bottom tab bar.

title: Quiet rail of corner cells
summary: QR + CWS composed: the 56→72px quiet rail is a vertical stack of 4 corner-widget cells. Each cell carries live status (saved-count, PHP version, blueprint count, warning count) AND is the only door to its concern. One 360px loud pane opens between rail and iframe; on mobile the rail rotates to a bottom tab bar and the pane becomes a bottom sheet.

title: Quiet rail · Mobile parity
summary: QR (quiet-rail-to-loud IA) + MOB (mobile-first wider-phone) composed so the mobile design is the canonical IA and the desktop is literally a 'wider phone'. A 5-dock quiet rail lives on the bottom on mobile (tab bar) and rotates 90° to the left edge on desktop. The same secondary surface is a sheet with three detents (peek 35% / mid 65% / full 85%) — vertical on mobile, horizontal on desktop. A visible detent gauge on the seam shows current detent, snapping not dragging. Composes QR + MOB primarily; threads CWS (corner Sites widget), DEV (slim top capsule), and VEN (≤5 items per pane) without overloading.

title: Four corners · Sharp roles
summary: All persistent chrome lives in four corner widgets, each with a sharp non-overlapping role and a live-status mirror. Top-left = Sites (current site + state, door to library/saved/new). Top-right = Settings + fullscreen (WP/PHP pills + explicit F button). Bottom-left = Logs (tiny live error count + tail). Bottom-right = Site Manager (Files/Blueprint/Database). Each widget is its sole door and expands inward from its own corner into a single glass panel anchored to that corner. No top bar. No rail. No drawer.

title: Rich Sites Widget · Slim Top Bar
summary: One frosted-glass Sites widget anchored to the top-left of the iframe carries the entire launcher — current site status, four saved playgrounds, six new-site sources, three featured tiles, and the only door to the 43-blueprint library. Everything that isn't Sites lives in a single 46px slim DevTools-style top bar. The widget is rich; the bar is minimal. Composes CWS + DEV + GLS + VEN + MOB.

title: Atlas · Corner door home
summary: SPT + CWS. Idle is a pan/zoom Figma-style atlas of saved playgrounds (every site has a stable 2D position; pan/zoom is the only navigation). Focus a tile and the iframe takes the canvas; a frosted corner widget at bottom-left becomes the canonical door back to the atlas — it shows the current site's live status AND a tiny minimap of the atlas with the focused site highlighted. Atlas mode and Site mode are mode-disjoint with one shared door.

title: Cartograph · Atlas beside the iframe
summary: SPT + SR composed: saved playgrounds occupy stable 2D positions on a pannable atlas; that atlas IS the persistent secondary pane of a smart-resize layout. Whichever pane you touch grows; the other recedes. The atlas never disappears — the periphery of saved sites stays visible while you work, like a sidebar that happens to be a map. Snap ratios 25/75, 35/65, 50/50, 65/35, 100/0; momentum gauge etched into the seam; one slim 40px DEV-style top bar; no left rail, no right drawer; one Sites cluster (the atlas pane) and one minimap cluster (the corner mini-atlas inside the iframe pane when the atlas is collapsed).

title: Ventura Sidebar · Smart Resize
summary: A Ventura-style left sidebar of 5 categories (Live · Sites · Settings · Blueprint · Logs) is the only chrome. Smart-resize governs the sidebar↔iframe seam: at rest the sidebar is 280px (Ventura-faithful); touch the iframe and it collapses to a 56px tinted rail; touch a category and the sidebar grows to 30% with the iframe ceding. A momentum gauge is etched into the seam showing 56/1384 · 280/1160 · 432/1008 · 720/720 · 1008/432 · 1440/0 snap detents. The five categories also serve as the canonical Site Manager tabs — same surface, one door.

title: Three Glass Surfaces · Strict Tiers
summary: GLS + 3LD strict: only three glass surfaces are ever visible — top capsule (8 px blur), bottom dock (18 px blur), right pill (36 px blur). Each lives at its own quantified depth tier. Iframe is the only solid surface; everything else is one of those three or hidden. Depth is purely decorative hierarchy, never a click target.

title: Depth-Decay Tabs
summary: A single browser-style tab strip is the only persistent chrome. Each tab is a saved Playground. The active tab is sharp at z0 (0px blur); adjacent tabs recede to z1 (8px blur); distant tabs to z2 (18px blur). Depth is decoration — every tab stays clickable — but the eye instantly knows where you are. The URL field lives inside the active tab (browser-of-browsers metaphor). The Site Manager slides up as the single secondary surface. Composes TAB + 3LD with DEV and MOB.

title: DevTools Glass
summary: DEV + GLS. A 44 px slim DevTools-style top bar and a right-side DevTools dock — both rendered as quantified frosted glass tinted from the iframe. The iframe is the only solid surface. Status pill, URL bar, save-state, dock tabs, gutters, code editor head, log levels — every chrome surface is glass at one of three quantified blur tiers (8 / 18 / 36 px). No two-row chrome, no depth-as-control, no terminal echo of the visual UI.

title: Corners on desktop · Status strip on mobile
summary: Mobile-first MOB + CWS composition: the same four corner widgets (TL Sites, TR Settings+Fullscreen, BL Logs, BR Site Manager) live as floating corner pills on desktop and as a horizontal four-cell status strip across the top on mobile. The IA is the same — four sole-door widgets that each show live status and expand into one secondary panel anchored to that widget. The transformation is purely geometric: corners on a wide canvas become a single row on a narrow one. Live status content survives intact: Sites shows current site + state, Settings shows WP/PHP versions, Logs shows error count, Site Manager shows focused tab. The expand-from-anchor behavior survives too — a tap on the mobile status cell expands a bottom sheet that points back at the cell it came from.

title: Bottom-rail everywhere · Mobile-first quiet rail
summary: MOB + QR composed so the quiet rail is anchored to the bottom edge of the viewport at every width. Mobile is the canonical IA: a 5-dock bottom tab bar with a peek-strip above it. Desktop borrows the same anchor — the rail does not rotate to the side; it stays a bottom tab bar at 1440 px too. The 'loud' surface is a single bottom sheet rising from the rail at three snap detents (peek 40 % · mid 70 % · full 92 %). The iframe is always the wallpaper above the rail; the rail's vertical position is invariant; only the sheet's height changes. This is QR's swap-by-dock IA preserved verbatim, but composed so MOB's bottom anchor is the source of truth and the desktop is literally a wider phone — same gestures, same anchor, same detent vocabulary.

title: Sites widget · Smart-resize tiles everywhere else
summary: The only persistent chrome is a frosted Sites widget anchored top-left. Everything else — address bar, save state, Files, Blueprint, Database, Logs, Settings — lives as smart-resize tiles in a mosaic. The iframe occupies the largest tile by default; whichever tile you touch grows by SR (snap 35/65, 50/50, 60/40, 75/25). No top bar, no rail, no drawer — just one widget and a living mosaic.

title: Floating Capsule · Smart-resize Secondary
summary: A single floating frosted-glass capsule replaces the top bar entirely. Below it the iframe smart-resizes with one secondary pane. No other persistent chrome. The capsule and the seam gauge are the only floating UI; everything else lives inside one of the two panes.

title: Notion document · Corner widget Sites
summary: Each playground is a Notion-style document. The iframe is a block; the blueprint is a block; files/database/logs/settings are blocks. You scroll the document; there is no Site Manager pane and no top bar. The only persistent chrome is a single frosted corner-widget Sites picker at top-left that doubles as live status of the current document. Click it to switch documents; press F on the iframe block to fullscreen-promote it. Document blocks have hover toolbars (drag handle, ⋮ menu, collapse) like a real Notion page; the iframe block has a focus-mode that re-flows the document around it.
